tuv.seg: Phatlalatso ya Qetello ya Selemo ya 2019

Abbreviations and Acronyms. Abbreviations and acronyms like “USA” or “ICE” should be avoided in the source text because they may be read in a way that does not coincide with their spelling. Additionally, there may be multiple accurate readings for a single abbreviation. For example, the acronym “ICE” could be pronounced “I-C-E” or as a single word.

Dikgutsufatso le mantswe a kgutsufaditsweng. Dikgutsufatso le mantswe a kgutsufaditsweng tse kang "USA" kapa "ICE" di lokela ho qojwa sengolweng-sa-mathomo hobane di ka balwa ka tsela e sa dumellaneng le mopeleto wa tsona. Ho feta moo, ho ka ba le mekgwa ya ho bala e mengata e nepahetseng bakeng sa kgutsufatso e le nngwe. Mohlala, kgutsufatso ya lentswe "ICE" e ka bitswa "I-C-E" kapa e le lentswe le le leng.
{ $actionType }<playIcon></playIcon> did they accurately speak the sentence?
{ $actionType }<playIcon></playIcon> na ba buile polelo eo ka nepo?
{ $actionType } <recordIcon></recordIcon> ebe o bala polelo eo o hweleditse
{ $actionType } romela ha e se e loketse
Add a language you want to contribute to
Kenya puo eo o batlang ho kenya letsoho ho yona
Add as much information as you can about the language. This could include:
Eketsa dintlha tse ngata kamoo o ka kgonang ka puo. Sena se ka kenyelletsa:
Ho kenya dipuo tse tlo sebetswa
addLink>Kenya dipolelo tse ding hona jwale!</addLink>
Kenya mokgwa wa ho bua o motjha o etseditsweng wena"{ $inputValue }"

All sentences you submit must be under <wikipediaLink>Public Domain (CC-0) license</wikipediaLink>. To support the inclusion of work not under public licence, we have created a <cc0WaiverLink>Contributions Agreement template</cc0WaiverLink> for works where the copyright owner would like to contribute the material to Common Voice.

Dipolelo tsohle tseo o di romelletseng di tlameha ho ba tlasa <wikipediaLink>laecence ya Setjhaba (CC-0)</wikipediaLink>. Ho tshehetsa ho kenyelletsa ha mosebetsi o seng tlasa laesense ya setjhaba, re entse</cc0WaiverLink>mohlala wa Tumellano ya Menehelo<cc0WaiverLink>bakeng sa mesebetsi moo monga ditokelo tsa sengolwa a ka ratang ho tlatsetsa ka thepa ho Common Voice.

All voice clips in the dataset are scrubbed of personally identifying information. When a contributor provides demographic data via their profile, that information is de-identified from their voice clips before being bundled for download in the dataset and is never made public on their profile page.

Kaofela dikotwana tsa mantswe tse ka hara pokello ya data di hlwekiswa ho tlosa dintlha tsa botho. Ha mofani a fana ka data ya palo ya batho ka profaele ya hae, dintlha tseo di hlwekiswa ho tlosa dintlha tsa botho pele di kopanngwa mmoho bakeng sa download ka ho pokello ya data mme ha di na ho phatlalatswa leqepheng la profaele la bona.

Anonymized user data like age, gender, and accent helps improve the audio data used to train the accuracy of speech recognition engines. Your username and email will never be associated with your submitted data, and you can choose whether to make your username public or anonymous.

Dintlha tsa mosebedisi tse sa tsebahaleng jwaloka dilemo, bong, le tsela ya puo di thusa ho ntlafatsa data ya audio e sebediswang ho kwetlisa nepahalo ya dienjene tse elellwang puo. Lebitso la mosebedisi la hao le imeile di ke ke tsa amahangwa le dintlha tse romelletsweng ya hao, mme o ka kgetha na o etsa lebitso la mosebedisi la hao le be phatlalatsa kapa le se ke la tsebahala.

A quiet background hubbub is OK, but we don’t want additional voices that may cause a machine algorithm to identify words that are not in the written text. If you can hear distinct words apart from those of the text, the clip should be rejected. Typically this happens where the TV has been left on, or where there is a conversation going on nearby.

Lerata le tlase le kamorao le nepahetse, empa ha re batle mantswe a mang a ka etsang hore algorithm ya motjhini e tsebe mantswe a sa ngolwang sengolweng. Haeba o utlwa mantswe a kgethehileng ntle le ho a sengolweng, sekotwana se tlameha ho hanwa. Ka tlwaelo hona ho etsahala moo TV e siilweng e butswe, kapa moo ho nang le moqoqo o ntseng o tswelapele haufi.
O wa tla? [o bitswa ke e mong]
A voice clip is marked "valid" when a user gives it a Yes vote.
Clip ya lentswe e tshwauwa "e nepahetse" ha mosebedisi a e fa kgetho ya Ee.
Avoid too much background noise - it should be easy to hear you.
Qoba lerata la kamorao le lengata haholo - ho lokela ho ba bonolo ho o utlwa.

Be cautious before rejecting a clip on the ground that the reader has mispronounced a word, has put the stress in the wrong place, or has apparently ignored a question mark. There are a wide variety of pronunciations in use around the world, some of which you may not have heard in your local community. Please provide a margin of appreciation for those who may speak differently from you.

Eba hlokolosi pele o hana sekotwana ka lebaka la hore mmadi ha bitsa lentswe hantle, o behile kgatello ya maikutlo sebakeng se fosahetseng, kapa ho bonahala a iphapanyeditse letswaho-potso. Hona le ditsela tse fapaneng tsa ho bitsa mantswe tse sebediswang lefatshe ka bophara, tse ding tseo o ka tswang o so di utlwe setjhabeng sa heno. Ka kopo fana ka moedi wa kananelo ho ba ka buang ka mokgwa o fapaneng le wa hao.
[‘Beret’ is OK whether with stress on the first syllable (UK) or the second (US)]
['Beret' e lokile ebang ke ka kgatello ya lentswe la pele (UK) kapa la bobeli (US)]
<bold>Re thuse</bold> ho fumana mantswe a mang
Aha sepheo se etseditsweng wena feela
Why an email? We may need to contact you in the future about changes to the dataset, an email provides us a point of contact.
<b>Hobaneng imeile?<b> Re ka hloka ho ikopanya le oena ka moso ka diphetoho tse ho pokello ya data, imeile e re fa mokgwa wa ho ikopanya le wena.
By editing your goal, you may lose your existing progress.
Ka ho hlophisa sepheo sa hao, o ka lahlehelwa ke tswelopele ya hao e teng.
By opting in to receive emails you state that you are okay with Mozilla handling this info as explained in Mozilla’s <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>.
Ha o kgetha ho amohela di-email o bolela hore ha o na bothata ha Mozilla e sebetsana le dintlha tsena jwalo kaha ho hlalositswe ho Mozilla <privacyLink>Leano la Lekunutu</privacyLink>.
You agree to not attempt to determine the identity of speakers in the Common Voice dataset
<b> O dumela<b> ho se leke ho fumana boitsebiso ba dibui tse ka ho pokello ya data ya Common Voice.
By providing some information about yourself, the audio data you submit to Common Voice will be more useful to Speech Recognition engines that use this data to improve their accuracy.
Ka ho fana ka dintlha tse ding ka wena, data ya audio yeo o e romellang ho Common Voice e tla ba molemo haholo ho dienjene tsa Temoho ya Puo tse sebedisang data ena ho ntlafatsa nepahalo ya tsona.
By using Common Voice, you agree to our <termsLink>Terms</termsLink> and <privacyLink>Privacy Notice</privacyLink>
Ka ho sebedisa Common Voice, o dumellana le <termsLink>Dipehelo</termsLink> le <privacyLink>Tsebiso ya Lekunutu</privacyLink>
Ha o kgone ho etsa qeto?
Change your email via Settings under Login Identity
Fetola imeile ya hao ka Ditlhophiso tlasa Boitsebiso ba ho Kena
Check if your language is listed on the Common Voice Languages page. If not, fill out this form and we’ll send you a handy email explaining how to get your language set up.
Sheba na puo ya hao e thathahamisitswe ho leqephe la Dipuo la Common Voice. Haeba ho se jwalo, tlatsa foromo ena mme re tla o romella imeile e molemo e hlalosang hore na o ka theha puo ya hao jwang.
{ $clipCount } voice clips, total archive size { $size }. Expires { $expires }.
{ $clipCount } di-clip tsa mantswe, ka kakaretso di lekane { $size }. E fellwa ke nako { $expires }.
Collecting sentences from the public domain, or writing new ones for the public domain.
Ho bokella dipolelo ho tswa sebakeng sa setjhaba, kapa ho ngola tse ntjha bakeng sa phatlalatso ya setjhaba.
Common Voice data plus all other voice datasets above.
Data ya Common Voice le dipokello tsa data ding kaofela tse ka hodimo.
Pokello ya Data ya Common Voice

Common Voice is a crowdsourcing platform, and the languages were all added by volunteers. We would love for you to add your language! <languageRequestLink>Ask about adding your language.</languageRequestLink>
Common Voice ke sethala sa batho ba bangata, mme dipuo kaofela di kentswe ke baithaopi. Re ka rata hore o kenye puo ya hao!<languageRequestLink>Botsa ka ho kenya puo ya hao</languageRequestLink>
Common Voice is Mozilla's initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Common Voice ke mohato wa Mozilla wa ho thusa ho ruta metjhini hore na batho ba nnete ba bua jwang.
Common Voice is part of Mozilla's initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Common Voice ke karolo ya boikitlaetso ba Mozilla ba ho thusa ho ruta metjhini hore na batho ba nnete ba bua jwang.
Common Voice is the world’s largest publicly available, multi-language voice dataset.
Common Voice ke pokello ya data ya mantswe e kgolo ka ho fetisisa lefatsheng e fumanehang phatlalatsa, e nang le dipuo tse ngata.

Common Voice recordings are used by academics, small businesses, and voice recognition enthusiasts to help train and grow publicly available resources like voice models.
Can you let us know why you would like your recordings deleted?

Direkoto tsa Common Voice Di sebediswa ke barutehi, dikhoebo tse nyane, le batho ba chesehelang ho tseba mantswe ho thusa ho kwetlisa le ho hodisa disebediswa tse fumanehang phatlalatsa joaloka mefuta ya mantswe.
A na o ka re tsebisa hore na ke hobaneng ha o batla hore direkoto tsa hao di hlakolwe?
Ho kenya letsoho ha setjhaba le ho etsa diqeto.
Buka ya ho bapala ya setjhaba
Content available under a <licenseLink>Creative Commons license</licenseLink>
Ditaba di fumaneha tlasa <licenseLink>laesense ya Creative Commons</licenseLink>
Contributors record voice clips by reading from a bank of donated sentences.
Batshehetsi ba rekota di-clip tsa mantswe ka ho bala ho tswa bankeng ya dipolelo tse fanweng.
<coquiLink>Coqui</coquiLink> is dedicated to open speech technology. Their projects include deep learning based STT and TTS engines.
<coquiLink>Coqui</coquiLink> e inehetse ho thekenoloji ya puo e bulehileng. Merero ya bona e kenyelletsa boithuto bo tebileng bo thehilweng ho enjene tsa STT le TTS.
Ha e ya kgona ho kenya puo
Could not change settings. Please try again.
Ha e kgone ho fetola ditlhophiso. Ka kopo leka hape.
Ha e ya kgona ho tlosa puo
Could not remove language: language not found
Ha e kgone ho tlosa puo: puo ha e fumanehe
{ $countOfReviewed } sentences are already reviewed. Great job!
{ $countOfReviewed } dipolelo di se di hlahlobilwe. Mosebetsi o motle!
{ $countOfUnreviewed } sentences are unreviewed. If you want, you can also review your sentences now before submitting them.
{ $countOfUnreviewed } ya dipolelo ha ya hlahlojwa. Haeba o batla, o ka boela wa hlahloba dipolelo tsa hao hona jwale pele o di romela.
Etsa sepheo se etseditsweng wena feela
Creating opportunities for a diversity of people to contribute to Common Voice ensures the dataset serves as many people as possible. We’ve created resources and templates that you can use!

Ho theha menyetla ya hore batho ba fapaneng ba kenye letsoho ho Common Voice ho netefatsa hore pokello ya data e sebeletsa batho ba bangata kamoo ho ka kgonehang. Re entse disebediswa le mehlala eo o ka e sebedisang!
Don't see your language on Common Voice yet?
Na ha o so bone puo ya hao ho Common Voice?
Don’t see your language reflected in the Dataset? To request a language head over to our Languages page.
Ha o bone puo ya hao e hlaha ho Dataset? Ho kopa puo eya ho leqephe la rona la Dipuo.
Download-a Sephuthelwana sa Polokelo ya Data
Download the Single Word Target Segment
Daonlouda karolo e lebisitsweng ya lentswe le le leng
Do you have ideas on how we can make the Common Voice dataset better? Let us know on Discourse
O na le maikutlo a hore na re ka etsa jwang hore pokello ya data ya Common Voice e be betere? Re tsebise ho Discourse
Do you want to Speak, Listen or both?
Na o batla ho Bua, ho Mamela, kapa di le pedi?
During contribution submission feedback will be skipped after clicking 'Submit'. Contribution will continue directly with the next set of 5 recordings or validations.
Nakong ya thomello ya tlatsetso maikutlo a tla fetwa kamora ho penya 'Romella'. Tlatsetso e tla tswela pele ka kotloloho ka sete e latelang ya direkoto tse 5 kapa tsa dinetefatso.

Each entry in the dataset consists of a unique MP3 and corresponding text file. Many of the { $total } recorded hours in the dataset also include demographic metadata like age, sex, and accent that can help train the accuracy of speech recognition engines. The dataset currently consists of { $valid } validated hours in { $languages } languages, but we’re always adding more voices and languages. Take a look at our Languages page to request a language or start contributing.

Ntho e nngwe le e nngwe e kentsweng ka hara pokello ya data e na le MP3 e ikgethileng le faele ya sengolwa e tsamaellanang le yona. Bongata ba { $total } dihora tse rekotilweng kahara pokello ya data di boetse di kenyelletsa metadata ya dintlha tsa palo ya batho jwaloka dilemo, bong le tsela ya puo tse ka thusang ho kwetlisa nepahalo ya dienjene tse elellwang puo. Pokello ya data hajwale e na le { $valid }dihora tse netefaditsweng ka { $languages } dipuo, empa re dula re eketsa mantswe le dipuo tse ding. Sheba Leqephe la Dipuo la rona ho kopa puo kapa ho qala ho tlatsetsa.
<emailFragment>Sign up</emailFragment> to our mailing list to learn how you can take part in campaigns, events and co-design features on Common Voice.
<emailFragment>Ingodise</emailFragment> ho lenane la rona la mangolo ho ithuta hore na o ka nka karolo jwang matsholong, diketsahalong le dikarolo tsa moralo o kopanetsweng ho Common Voice.
Email is already used for a different account
Imeile e se ntse e sebediswa bakeng sa ak'haonte e fapaneng
Enter email to send a sign up link
Kenya imeile ho romela hokahanyo ya ho ngodisa
Error while fetching rejected sentences. Please try again.
Phoso ha re lata dipolelo tsa hao tse hannweng. Ka kopo leka hape.
Error while fetching your sentences. Please try again.
Phoso ha re lata dipolelo tsa hao. Ka kopo leka hape.

Expert linguists can also help, try to reach out to local linguistic universities (both teachers and students) and see if they can help to gather a diverse set of sound-diverse sentences in your language.

Ditsebi tse hlwahlwa tsa dipuo le tsona di ka thusa, leka ho ikopanya le diunivesithi tsa lehae tsa botsebi ba dipuo bo hlwahlwa (bobedi matichere le baithuti) mme o bone na di ka thusa ho bokella mefuta-futa ya dipolelo tse nang le medumo e mefuta-futa puong ya hao.
Failed to delete selected sentences… Please try again!
E hlolehile ho hlakola dipolelo tse kgethilweng...Ka kopo leka hape!
Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required.
Dibaka tse tshwauweng ka naledi (*) di a hlokahala.
Filtered sentences due to requirements failing (please submit fixed versions as new sentences):
Dipolelo tse siuweng ka lebaka la ditlhoko tse hlolehileng (ka kopo fana ka mefuta e tsitsitseng e le dipolelo tse ntjha):
Finding existing sentences in the Public Domain
Ho fumana dipolelo tse teng Setjhabeng

Find others who care. That could be universities, language schools, advocacy groups or data science communities. Reach out and explain clearly how they can help and why. <outreachTemplates>See our template outreach emails</outreachTemplates>.

Batla ba bang ba tsotellang. Bao e ka ba diunivesithi, dihlopha tsa babuelelli kapa dihlopha tsa mahlale a data. Ikopanye le bona mme o hlalose hantle na bona ba ka thusa jwang le hobaneng. <outreachTemplates>Sheba mehlala ya rona ho di-imeile tsa phihlello</outreachTemplates>.

First, check if your language <languageLink>already exists</languageLink>. If it isn’t, you can <languageRequestLink>ask about adding your language</languageRequestLink>. There are two stages. Translating the site, and collecting sentences

Pele, sheba haeba puo ya hao <languageLink>e se ntse e le teng</languageLink>. Haeba e le sieo, o ka <languageRequestLink>botsa ka ho kenyelletsa puo ya hao</languageRequestLink>. Ho na le mekgahlelo e mmedi. Ho toloka dipuo setsing sa marang-rang, le ho bokella dipolelo.

Foreign letters. Letters must be valid in the language being spoken. For example, “ж” is a letter in the Russian alphabet but is never used in English and so should never appear in any English source text.

Ditlhaku tse molata. Ditlhaku di tlameha ho ba tse sebetsang puuong e buuwang. Mohlala, “ж” ke tlhaku e teng ho ditlhaku tsa Se-Russia empa ha e so ka ba e sebediswa Sekgoweng mme ha ya lokela ho hlaha ho sengolwa-sa-mathomo se feng kapa se feng sa Sekgowa.

For every voice clip donated, and every audio clip validated, your account dashboards are updated to reflect your latest progress in each language you contribute to. Yes, you can contribute to more than one!

Use dashboards to track your stats, see how you're doing alongside others in the community, and set daily or weekly contribution goals.

Bakeng sa sekotwana se seng le se seng sa lentswe se fanweng, le sekotwana se seng le se seng sa audio se netefaditsweng, di-dashboard tsa ak'haonte ya hao di ya ntjhafatswa ho bontsha tswelopele ya hao ya morao-rao ho puo e nngwe le e nngwe yeo o tlatsetsang ho yona. E, o ka tlatsetsa ho tse fetang bonngwe!

Sebedisa di-dashboard ho latela dipalo-palo tsa hao, sheba na o sebetsa jwang papisong le ba bang setjhabeng, mme ipehele dipheo tsa tlatsetso tsa letsatsi kapa tsa beke.
For public domain text not available online, you could use academic referencing style e.g Harvard style "Jess (2021) My Public licence poems"
Bakeng sa mongolo wa setjhaba o sa fumaneheng marang-rang, o ka sebedisa mokgwa wa ditshupiso tsa thuto, mohlala, mokgwa wa Harvard "Jess (2021) dithothokiso tsa ka tsa laecence ya setjhaba"

For these launched languages the website has been successfully <localizationGlossaryLink>localized</localizationGlossaryLink>, and has enough <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>sentences collected</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink> to allow for ongoing <speakLink>Speak</speakLink> and <listenLink>Listen</listenLink> contributions.

Bakeng sa dipuo tsena tse thakgotsweng websaete<localizationGlossaryLink>e entswe selehae</localizationGlossaryLink> ka katleho, mme e na le <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>dipolelo tse lekaneng tse bokelletsweng</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>ho dumella ntshetsopele ya ho<speakLink>Bua</speakLink> le ho <listenLink>Mamela</listenLink> ditlatsetso.
<githubLink>NVIDIA NeMo</githubLink>™ is an <docsLink>open-source toolkit</docsLink> for researchers developing state-of-the-art conversational AI models.
<githubLink>NVIDIA NeMo</githubLink>™ ke <docsLink>sesebediswa sa mohlodi o bulehileng</docsLink> bakeng sa bafuputsi ba hlahisang di-model AI tse qoqang tsa boemo bo phahameng haholo.
<governanceLink>Bala haholwanyane ka moo re buswang ka teng</governanceLink>
Government proceedings, books and articles are also great however since the text tends to be a little more formal they are less of a priority.
Ditshebetso tsa mmuso, dibuka le dingodilweng le tsona di ntle haholo empa kaha sengolwa se na le ho ba semmuso haholwanyane ha di bohlokwa haholo.
Great! How many clips a week?
E ntle! Ke di-clip tse kae ka beke?
Great! How many clips per day?
E ntle! Ke di-clip tse kae ka letsatsi?
E ntle!<recordIcon></recordIcon> Rekota clip ya hao e latelang
Great work!<playIcon></playIcon> Listen again when you're ready
Mosebetsi o motle!<playIcon></playIcon> Mamela hape ha o se o itokisitse
Na o badile Dipehelo tsa rona?
Having an account is not required to contribute, though it is helpful.
Ho ba le ak'haonte ha ho hlokahale bakeng sa ho kenya letsoho, le hoja ho thusa.
Having a profile is not required to contribute though it is helpful, see why below.
Ho ba le profaele ha ho hlokahale ho kenya letsoho leha ho le molemo, bona hore na hobaneng mona ka tlase.
Help Common Voice reach { $hours } hours in a language with a personal goal
Thusa Common Voice e fihlele { $hours } dihora puong ka sepheo sa hao
Help create Common Voice’s first target segment in { $locale }
Thusa ho etsa karolo ya pele ya Common Voice ka { $locale }
Help reach { $hours } hours in { $language } with a personal goal
Thusa hore re fihlelle { $hours } dihora ho { $language } ka sepheo sa hao
Help teach machines how real people speak, donate your voice at { $link }
Thusa ho ruta metjhini hore na batho ba nnete ba bua jwang, fana ka lentswe la hao ho { $link }
Help us build a community around voice technology, stay in touch via email.
Re thuse ho aha setjhaba mabapi le thekenoloji ya lentswe, dula re buisana ka imeile.
Help us build a high quality, publicly open dataset
Re thuse ho aha pokello ya data ya boleng bo hodimo, e bulehileng phatlalatsa
Help us by reviewing sentences for correctness according to the guidelines.
Re thuse ka ho hlahloba dipolelo bakeng sa nepahalo ho latela ditataiso.
Help us by writing or collecting Public Domain sentences.
Re thuse ka ho ngola kapa ho bokella dipolelo tsa Setjhaba tse phatlaladitsweng.
Help us find more voices, share your goal
Re thuse ho fumana mantswe a mang hape, arolelana ka sepheo sa hao.
Help us find others to donate their voice!
Re thuse ho fumana ba bang ho nehela ka mantswe a bona!
Here are some tips to find sentences:
Ditlhahiso ke tsena tsa ho fumana dipolelo:
Here are the links to download your ZIP files.
Dihokelo tsa ho download-a difaele tsa ZIP tsa hao ke tsena.
Letsoho la hae le ne le phahamisitswe.
{ $hours } dihora tse netefaditsweng ho fihlela jwale!
Diqeto tsa morero di etswa jwang?
How can I get the Common Voice data?
Ebe nka fumana data ya Common Voice jwang?
How can we effectively grow a language on Common Voice?
Re ka hodisa puo ka katleho jwang ho Common Voice?
How does Common Voice calculate hours?
Common Voice e bala dihora jwang?
Ebe localization ya sebaka sa marang rang e etswa jwang?
How do I access and use the dataset?
Ebe ke fumana le ho sebedisa pokello ya data jwang?
How do I add a language?
How do I know whether to approve a voice clip?
Ke tseba jwang hore nka amohela clip ya lentswe?
How do I record a high quality voice clip?
Nka rekota clip ya lentswe ya boleng bo hodimo jwang?
How do I stay in touch?
How do you ensure anonymity and privacy of the people who donated their voices?
O etsa bonnete ba hore batho ba faneng ka mantswe a bona boitsebiso ba bona eba lekunutu jwang?
How would you describe your accent?
O ka hlalosa mokgwa wa hao wa ho bitsa mantswe jwang?
I am a non-native speaker and I speak with an accent, do you still want my voice?
Ke sebui se seng letswallwa mme ke bitsa mantswe ka mokgwa o fapaneng, na o ntse o batla lentswe la ka?
I confirm that these sentences are <wikipediaLink>public domain</wikipediaLink> and I have permission to upload them.
Ke netefatsa hore dipolelo tsena ke<wikipediaLink>tsa setjhaba</wikipediaLink> mme ke na le tumello ya ho di kenya.
I'd like to receive emails such as goal reminders, my progress updates and newsletters about Common Voice.
Ke kopa ho fumana di-imeile tse kang dikgopotso tsa sepheo, ditlaleho tsa tswelopele ya ka le dikoranta tse mabapi le Common Voice.
If a clip is rejected by 2 people, it is released in a different subset of the dataset.
Haeba clip e hanwa ke batho ba 2, e lokollelwa ka hara sehlopha se fapaneng sa pokello ya data.
If the recording breaks up, or has crackles, reject unless the entirety of the text can still be heard.
Haeba rekoto e robeha, kapa e e-na le modumo o hlabang, e hane ntle le haeba mongolo oohle o ntse o ka utluwa.
If the recording breaks up, or has crackles, reject unless the entirety of the text can still be heard.
Haeba rekoto e robeha, kapa e e-na le modumo o hlabang, e hane ntle le haeba mongolo oohle o ntse o ka utluwa.
If the sentence does not meet the above criteria, click the "Reject" button on the left. If you are unsure about the sentence, you may also skip it and move on to the next one.
Haeba polelo e sa fihlelle ditekanyetso tse ka hodimo, penya "Hana" konopo e ka ho le letshehadi. Haeba o sena bonnete ka polelo, o ka boela wa e tlola mme o fetele ho e latelang.
If the sentence does not meet the above criteria, click the "Reject" button. If you are unsure about the sentence, you may also skip it and move on to the next one.
Ha polelo e sa fihlelle ditekanyetso tse ka hodimo, penya konopo ya "Hana". Haeba o sena bonnete ka polelo, o ka boela wa e tlola mme o fetele ho e latelang.
If the sentence meets the criteria, click the "Approve" button.
Haeba polelo e kopana le ditekanyetso, tobetsa konopo ya "Amohela".
If the sentence meets the criteria, click the "Approve" button on the right.

Most of the data used by large companies isn’t available to the majority of people. We think that stifles innovation. So we’ve launched Common Voice, a project to help make voice recognition open and accessible to everyone.

Boholo ba data e sebediswang ke dikhamphani tse kgolo ha e fumanehe ho boholo ba batho. Re nahana hore hona ho sitisa ho etsa dintho tse ntjha. Ke ka hoo re qadileng Common Voice, morero wa ho thusa ho etsa hore thekenoloji e elellwang mantswe e bulelwe bohle.

Most of the data used by large companies isn’t available to the majority of people. We think that stifles innovation. So we’ve launched Project Common Voice, a project to help make voice recognition open to everyone.

Boholo ba data e sebediswang ke dikhamphani tse kgolo ha e fumanehe ho boholo ba batho. Re nahana hore hona ho sitisa ho etsa dintho tse ntjha. Ke ka hoo re qadileng Common Voice, morero wa ho thusa ho etsa hore thekenoloji e elellwang mantswe e bulelwe bohle.

Most recordings are of people talking in their natural voice. You can accept the occasional non-standard recording that is shouted, whispered, or obviously delivered in a ‘dramatic’ voice. Please reject sung recordings and those using a computer-synthesized voice.

Boholo ba direkoto ke tsa batho ba buang ka lentswe la bona la tlhaho. O ka amohela rekoto e seng maemong ya hwane le hwane e hweletsang, e hweshetsang kapa e hlakileng hore e entswe ka lentswe le nang le 'terama'. Ka kopo hana direkoto tse binwang le tse sebedisang lentswe le entsweng ka k'homphuta.

Most speech databases are trained with an overrepresentation of certain demographics which results in a bias towards <articleLink>male and middle class</articleLink>. Accents and dialects that tend to be under-represented in training data sets are typically associated with groups of people who are already marginalised. Many machines also struggle to understand female voices. This is why in our voice database we want variety!

Boholo ba di-database tsa puo di kwetlisitswe ka boemedi bo fetelletseng ba dintlha tsa palo ya batho e itseng e leng se bakang leeme ho <articleLink>banna le ba maemo a ditjhelete a mahareng</articleLink>. Ditsela tsa puo le maleme a atisang ho ba le boemedi bo fokolang kahare ho dipokello tsa data tsa kwetliso ka tlwaelo a amahanngwa le dihlopha tsa batho tse se ntse di kgethollwa. Metjhini e mengata e boela e thatafallwa ke ho utlwa mantswe a basadi. Ke ka hoo re batlang mefuta e fapaneng ho database ya mantswe!
Mozilla Common Voice is an initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Mozilla Common Voice ke mohato wa ho thusa ho ruta metjhini hore na batho ba nnete ba bua jwang.

Mozilla Common Voice is made possible by a diverse community of activists, linguists, data scientists, academics and software engineers from all over the world. The project is stewarded by the Mozilla Foundation.

Mozilla Common Voice e entswe ke mefuta-futa ya ditjhaba tsa baitseki, ditsebi tsa puo, boramahlale ba data, barutehi le dienjiniere tsa software ho tswa hohle lefatsheng. Morero ona o tsamaiswa ke Motheo wa Mozilla.

Mozilla is dedicated to keeping the web open and accessible for everyone. To do that we need to empower web creators through projects like Common Voice. As voice technologies proliferate beyond niche applications, we believe they must serve all users equally. That means investing in more languages and accommodating diverse accents and demographics when building and testing voice technologies. Common Voice is a public resource available to everyone and Mozilla teams and developers around the world are already using it on our own projects as well.

Mozilla e ikemiseditse ho boloka marang-rang a butswe ebile a fumaneha ho bohle. Ho etsa jwalo re hloka ho matlafatsa baetsi ba marang-rang ka merero e kang Common Voice. Ha dithekenoloji tsa lentswe di ntse di kenella tshebetsong ho feta, re dumela hore di tlameha ho sebeletsa basebedisi bohle ka ho lekana. Hona ho bolela ho tsetela dipuong tse ngata le ho amohela ditsela tsa puo tse fapaneng le batho ba fapaneng ha ho hahwa ​​​​le ho lekola dithekenoloji tsa lentswe. Common Voice ke sesebediswa sa setjhaba se fumanehang ho bohle, mme dihlopha tsa Mozilla le dihahi tsa marang-rang tsa lefatshe ka bophara di se ntse di e sebedisa le ho merero ya rona.

Mozilla’s open source voice recognition engine Deep Speech can be used to build speech recognition applications. Read our <githubLink>Github overview</githubLink> or join the <discourseLink>DeepSpeech Discourse</discourseLink> to learn how to get started.

Enjene e bitswang Deep Speech e elellwang lentswe e buletsweng bohle ya Mozilla e ka sebediswa ho haha di-application tse elellwang puo. Bala <githubLink>kakaretso ya Github</githubLink>kapa kenela <discourseLink>DeepSpeech Discourse</discourseLink> ho ithuta hore na o qala jwang.
O hloka thuso ka mokgwa wa ho bua?
O hloka thuso ka mefuta e fapaneng?
O hloka ho downlad-a data ya hao?

Now that you know a little bit more about Common Voice, why not try it out? Click on the microphone icon to start reading sentences aloud.

If you prefer to review other people's voice contributions, click on the play icon. You’ll help confirm that recordings match the sentences written on screen.

Kaha jwale o tseba haholwanyane ka Common Voice, ke hobaneng o sa e leke? Penya letshwao la maekerofounu ho qala ho bala dipolelo ka lentswe le phahameng.

Haeba o kgetha ho hlahloba ditlatsetso tsa lentswe tsa batho ba bang, penya ho letshwao la bapala. O tla thusa ho netefatsa hore direkoto di dumellana le dipolelo tse ngotsweng screen-eng.

Now you can donate your voice to help us build an open-source voice database that anyone can use to make innovative apps for devices and the web. Read a sentence to help machines learn how real people speak. Check the work of other contributors to improve the quality. It’s that simple!

Hona jwale o ka fana ka lentswe la hao ho re thusa ho haha database ya lentswe e buletsweng bohle yeo mang kapa mang a ka e sebedisang ho etsa disebediswa tse ntjha tsa disebediswa le marang-rang. Bala polelo ho thusa metjhini ho ithuta kamoo batho ba phelang ba buang. Sheba mosebetsi wa bafani ba bang ho ntlafatsa boleng. Ho bonolo jwalo!

Numbers. There should be no digits in the source text because they can cause problems when read aloud. The way a number is read depends on context and might introduce confusion in the dataset. For example, the number “2409” could be accurately read as both “twenty-four zero nine” and “two thousand four hundred nine”.

Dinomoro. Ha hwa lokela hoba teng dinomoro ho sengolwa-sa-mathomo hobane di ka baka mathata ha di ballwa hodimo. Mokgwa wo nomoro e balwang o ipapisitse le moelelo mme o ka tlisa pherekanyo kahare ho pokello ya data. Mohlala, nomoro "2409" e ka balwa ka nepo e le "mashome a mane noto robong" le "dikete tse pedi makgolo a mane le metso e robong".

On the other hand, if you think that the reader has probably never come across the word before, and is simply making an incorrect guess at the pronunciation, please reject. If you are unsure, use the skip button.

Ka lehlakoreng le leng, haeba o nahana hore mohlomong mmali ha a e-s'o ka a kopana le lentswe pele, mme o mpa a nahana ka mokgwa o fosahetseng oa ho bitsa mantswe, ka kopo, hana. Haeba o sena bonnete, sebedisa konopo ya tlola.
Optionally join on our email list for updates and new information about the project.
Ka boikgethelo kena lenaneng la rona la mangolo bakeng sa ditlaleho le dintlha tse ntjha mabapi le morero.

Optionally submitted demographic data (e.g. age, gender, language, and accent) will never be made public on your profile, and will not be linked to your account in the dataset. Individual audio clips will be associated with demographic data for the purpose of more accurate analysis - for example, a researcher might want to target a training model to a specific demographic segment.

Dintlha tsa palo ya batho tse rometsweng ka boikgethelo (mohlala, dilemo, bong, puo, le tsela ya puo) di ke ke tsa phatlalatswa ho profaele ya hao, mme di ke ke tsa hokelwa ho ak'haonte ya hao kahara pokello ya data. Dikotwana tsa audio ka bonngwe di tla amahanngwa le dintlha tsa palo ya batho bakeng sa tlhahlobo e nepehahetseng - mohlala, mofuputsi a ka batla ho lebisa model o kwetlisang ho karolo e itseng ya palo ya batho.

Batho ba bang ba netefatsa di-clip tseo tsa mantswe.

O ka sheba dipolelo-tsa-mathomo tsa rona ho <githubLink>faele ya GitHub</githubLink>.

Common Voice is a publicly available voice dataset, powered by the voices of volunteer contributors around the world. People who want to build voice applications can use the dataset to train machine learning models.

At present, most voice datasets are owned by companies, which stifles innovation. Voice datasets also underrepresent: non-English speakers, people of colour, disabled people, women and LGBTQIA+ people. This means that voice-enabled technology doesn’t work at all for many languages, and where it does work, it may not perform equally well for everyone. We want to change that by mobilising people everywhere to share their voice.

Common Voice ke pokello ya data ya mantswe e fumanehang phatlalatsa, e matlafatswang ke mantswe a bafani ba ithaopileng lefatshe ka bophara. Batho ba batlang ho haha disebediswa tsa lentswe ba ka sebedisa pokello ya data ho kwetlisa di-model tsa ho ithuta ka motjhini.

Hajwale, boholo ba dipokello tsa data di ruilwe ke dikhamphani, tse kginang ho etswa ha dintho tse ntjha. Dipokello tsa data tsa mantswe di boela ha di emele hantle: batho ba sa buueng Sekgowa, batho ba mmala, batho ba nang le bokowa, basadi le batho ba LGBTQIA+. Hona ho bolela hore thekenoloji e sebetsang ka mantswe ha e sebetse ho hang bakeng sa dipuo tse ngata, mme le moo e sebetsang, ha e sebetse hantle ka tekano bakeng sa bohle. Re batla ho fetola hono ka ho bokella batho hohle hore ba arolelane ka mantswe a bona.
People come and contribute their voices.
Batho ba tla mme ba nehelane ka mantswe a bona.
Personal dashboards keep you up-to-date with individual and community progress.
Di-dashboard tsa hao di o boloka o le morao-rao mabapi le tswelopele ya motho ka mong le ya setjhaba.

Create an account if you don’t have one. Then, choose your language (‘Team’) and then choose the project, Common Voice. There will be files to translate. Click on one, then it shows words in English and a box to translate them.

Etsa ak'haonte haeba o se na yona. Ebe, o kgetha puo ya hao (‘Sehlopha’) mme ebe o kgetha morero, Common Voice . Ho tla ba le difaele tsa ho tolokwa. Penya ho e le nngwe, ebe e tla bontsha mantswe ka Sekgowa le lebokose ho ditoloka.

Punctuation. Special symbols and punctuation should only be included when absolutely necessary. For example, an apostrophe is included in English words like “don’t” and “we’re” and should be included in the source text, but it’s unlikely you’ll ever need a special symbol like “@” or “#.”

Matshwao a Puo. Matshwao a puo a kgethehileng a kenyelletswe feela ha ho hlokahala. Mohlala, sehonyetso se kenyelletswa mantsweng a sekgowa jwaloka "don't" le " we're" mme a tshwanela ho kenyelletswa ho sengolwa-sa-mathomo, empa ho ka se etsahale hore ebe o tla hloka matshwao a kgethehileng a kang "@" kapa "#".
[‘Raised’ in English is always pronounced as one syllable, not two]
[‘Ho phahamiswa’ ka Senyesemane kamehla ho bitswa jwalo ka senoko se le seng, eseng tse pedi]
Reading contractions that aren't actually there, such as "We're" instead of "We are", or vice versa.
Ho bala dikgakanyo tse siyo, jwalo ka "We're" ho fapana le "We are", kapa ka tsela e fapaneng.

Recording voice clips is an integral part of building our open dataset; some would say it's the fun part too.
Ho rekota di-ciip tsa lentswe ke karolo ya bohlokwa ya ho aha pokello ya data e bulehileng; ba bang ba ka re ke karolo e monate le yona.

Remember that we need permission to publish those sentences, so always ensure that the text belongs to the <wikipediaLink>public domain</wikipediaLink>. If there is no explicit indication, reach out to the person that the text belongs to and ask if they want to contribute their text to the Public Domain. If they want to, help them go through the <cc0WaiverLink>Contributions Agreement</cc0WaiverLink> process. If they do not want to contribute the text, then you are not allowed to use that text.

Hopola hore re hloka tumello ho phatlalatsa dipolelo tseo, kahoo kamehla etsa bonnete ba hore sengolwa ke <wikipediaLink>sa setjhaba se phatlalatsa</wikipediaLink>. Haeba ho se letho le bontshang ka ho hlaka, buisana le motho eo sengolwa e leng sa hae mme o botse hore na o batla ho tlatsetsa ka sengolwa sa hae ho Tsa Setjhaba. Haeba ba batla, ba thuse ho kena tshebetsong ya<cc0WaiverLink>Tumello ya Bafani</cc0WaiverLink>. Haeba ba sa batle ho tlatsetsa ka sengolwa, jwale ha wa dumellwa ho sebedisa sengolwa seo.

Sentences must be reviewed and accepted by two people to be included in Common Voice. You create guidelines for your language <languageGuidelinesLink>here</languageGuidelinesLink>. Sentences must be in the <licenseLink>public domain</licenseLink> and shorter than 15 words. You can ask the owner of a text to make it <waiverLink>CC0 using our waiver process</waiverLink>, and send to us on commonvoice@mozilla.com

Dipolelo di tlameha ho hlahlojwa le ho amohelwa ke batho ba babedi hore di kenyelletswe ho Common Voice. O etsa ditataiso tsa puo ya hao<languageGuidelinesLink> mona</languageGuidelinesLink>. Dipolelo di tlameha<licenseLink> ho ba tse ka bonwang ke setjhaba</licenseLink> mme di be ka tlase ho mantswe a 15. O ka kopa mong'a sengolwa hore a e etse<waiverLink> CC0 ka ho sebedisa mokgwa wa rona wa ho nyahlatsa</waiverLink>, mme a re romelle yona ho commonvoice@mozilla.com

Some languages have enormous variation in grammar, vocabulary and pronunciation. For this reason, we are <ctaLink>introducing ‘Variants’</ctaLink> in 2022. This gives communities a way to distinguish their languages within the larger dataset.

Dipuo tse ding di na le phapang e kgolo haholo ho sebopeho-puo, tlotlontswe le pitso ya mantswe. Ka lebaka lena, re <ctaLink>tsebisa 'Mefuta e fapaneng’</ctaLink> ka 2022. Hona ho fa ditjhaba mokgwa wa ho kgetholla dipuo tsa bona ka hara pokello ya data e kgolwanyane.
Someone asks for a language to be added.
Motho e mong o kopa hore a ekeletswe puo.

Ho bile le phoso ka reCAPTCHA. Ka kopo leka hape.
Sorry, Common Voice is running slowly. Thanks for your interest.
Tshwarelo, Common Voice e sebetsa butle. Re leboha thahasello ya hao.
Tshwarelo, ho na le ho hong ho fosahetseng
Speak in your normal voice! The way you speak is welcome here - we want your accent as it is, and we want your usual volume, style and intonation.

Bua ke lentswe la hao le tlwalehileng! Mokgwa oo o buang ka wona o amohelehile mona - re batla tsela ya hao ya puo e le jwalo, mme re batla bophahamo ba hao ba lentswe, setaele le modumo wa lentswe tse tlwaelehileng.

Speech is often the most natural way we communicate with each other and voice technologies are bringing that convenience to our computers and mobile devices. We want to empower developers to build amazing voice recognition applications like real-time translators and voice-enabled digital assistants. But right now most of the voice data required to build these kinds of apps is expensive and proprietary. We hope the Common Voice dataset will give developers what they need to innovate and make speech technology available in their own language.
To make voice recognition even more universal, we're collecting voice samples in widely spoken languages as well as those with a smaller population of speakers often underserved by commercial speech recognition services. Publishing a diverse dataset of voices will empower developers, entrepreneurs, and entire speech communities to address this gap themselves.

Puo hangata ke tsela ya tlhaho eo re buisanang mmoho ka eona mme dithekenoloji tsa lentswe di tlisa boiketlo boo ba puisano ho dikhomphuta le disebediswa tsa mohala tsa rona. Re batla ho matlafatsa batho ba ntlafatsang ho haha disebediswa tse hlollang tse elellwang lentswe jwaloka bafetoledi ba nako ya nnete le bathusi ba digital ba sebetsang ka lentswe. Empa hajwale boholo ba data ya lentswe e hlokahalang ho haha disebediswa tsa mofuta ona e ya tura mme e ruilwe ke batho ba itseng. Re tshepa hore pokello ya data ya Common Voice e tla fa bahahi ba marang-rang seo ba se hlokang ho qapa le ho etsa thekenoloji ya puo e fumanehe ka puo tseo e leng tsa bona.
Ho etsa hore thekenoloji e elellwang lentswe e pharalle le lefatshe, re ntse re bokella mehlala ya mantswe dipuong tse buuwang haholo mmoho le ho dipuo tse buuwang ke ditjhaba tse fokolang ka palo tseo hangata di nyenyefatswang ke dikgwebo tse fanang ka ditshebeletso tsa thekenoloji e elellwang puo.
Speech-to-text (STT) technologies convert voice data into text.
Dithekenoloji tsa Puo ho ya ho mongolo (STT) di fetolela data ya lentswe ho ba mongolo.
Arolela ho { $archiveCount } difaele tsa ZIP tse nang le

Do not reject clips where the speaker ‘has an accent’ that is different to your own - this is important for voice recognition to work better for everyone.
Se ke wa hana dikotwana moo mmui 'a nang le tsela ya puo' e fapaneng ho ya hao - hona ho bohlokwa bakeng sa hore temoho ya lentswe e sebetse betere bakeng sa bohle.
Get creative! Your language community will be unique, and these are just a few ways to get started.
E-ba le boiqapi! Puo ya heno e tla ikgetha, mme tsena ke ditsela tse seng kae feela tsa ho qalalella.

Sentence Collector is a tool for collecting and validating public domain sentences. You will need to create an account. Then, add your language to your Profile. You can Add sentences or Review previously-added sentences.

Swipe right to approve the sentence. Swipe left to reject it. Swipe up to skip it. Do not forget to submit your review!
Penya ka ho isa ho le letona ho amohela polelo. Penya ka ho isa ho le letshehadi ho e hana. Penya ka ho isa hodimo ho e feta. Se ke wa lebala ho fana ka tlhatlhobo ya hao!

Ho leka makgetlo a mmalwa ho bala lentswe.
Tatoeba is a large database of sentences, translations, and spoken audio for use in language learning. This download contains spoken English recorded by their community.
Tatoeba ke database e kgolo ya dipolelo, diphetolelo, le audio e builweng e sebediswang boithutong ba puo. Download ena e na le Sekgowa se builweng se rekotilweng ke setjhaba sa yona.

Thanks to contributions from over 259k people in over 50 languages, this data is being used to train speech-enabled applications to better respond to the human voice.
Ka lebaka la tlatsetso e tswang ho batho ba fetang 259k dipuong tse fetang 50, data ena e sebediswa ho kwetlisa disebediswa tse sebetsang ka puo ho araba hantle ho lentswe la motho.

The best sources you can look for are podcasts, transcripts, movie scripts and anything that potential can contain everyday conversations.
Mehlodi e metle eo o ka e batlang ke di-podcasts, dingodilweng, dingodilweng tsa difilimi le eng kapa eng e ka kgonang ho ba dipuisano tsa letsatsi le letsatsi.
Notshi e ile ya feta ka lebelo.

The Clip Graveyard consists of voice clips that didn't make it into the Common Voice dataset. Just like the dataset, the Clip Graveyard is available for download.
We would like to thank the following people and organizations for their help with the project:

Lebitla la dikotwana le na le dikotwana tsa mantswe tse sa kang tsa kenngwa ka hara pokello ya data ya Common Voice. Jwaloka pokello ya data, Lebitla la dikotwana le ya fumaneha bakeng sa download.
Re ka rata ho leboha batho le mekgatlo e latelang bakeng sa thuso ya bona ho morero ona.
The clip has disrespectful or offensive language.
Clip e na le puo e hlokang tlhompho kapa e hlabang.

The Common Voice dataset complements Mozilla’s open source voice recognition engine Deep Speech. The first version of Deep Speech was released in November 2017 and has continued to evolve ever since. Together with the Common Voice dataset, we believe this open source voice recognition technology should be available to everybody. It’s our hope these technologies will enable developers to build a wave of innovative products and services.

Pokello ya data ya Common Voice e dumellana le enjene e bitswang Deep Speech e elellwang lentswe ya Mozilla e buletsweng bohle. Mofuta wa pele wa Deep Speech o lokollotswe Pulungwana 2017 mme o tswetsepele ho fetoha ho tloha hono. Mmoho le pokello ya data ya Common Voice, re dumela hore thekenoloji ena ya lentswe e buletsweng bohle e fumanehe ho bohle. Ke tshepo ya rona hore dithekenoloji tsena di tla dumella dihahi tsa marang-rang ho haha leqhubu la dihlahiswa le ditshebeletso tse ntjha.
The Common Voice Dataset contains hundreds of thousands of voice samples that help developers build voice recognition tools.
Pokello ya data ya Common Voice e na le mehlala ya mantswe e dikete tse makgolo tse thusang batho ba ntlafatsang ho haha ​​​​disebediswa tsa ho elellwa lentswe.

The Common Voice dataset is an open and publicly available resource that can be used to train a wide variety of speech-enabled applications. To protect the security of our contributors, we ask everyone who downloads the Common Voice dataset to respect contributors’ privacy.
All voice clips in the dataset are scrubbed of personally identifying information. When you download the dataset, you agree to not attempt to determine the identity of any contributor. That means you cannot try to link information in the dataset to a contributor’s personal information. You may, however, use the dataset to train speech recognition, speaker recognition, or other applications, by, for instance, linking information in the dataset to other information already in the dataset.

Pokello ya data ya Common Voice ke sesebediswa se bulehileng mme se fumanehang phatlalatsa se ka sebediswang ho kwetlisa mefuta-futa ya disebediswa tse sebetsang ka puo. Ho sireletsa polokeho ya bafani ba rona, re kopa bohle ba download-ang pokello ya data ya Common Voice ba hlomphe boinotshi ba bafani.
Kaofela dikotwana tsa lentswe tse kahara pokello ya data di hlwekiswa ho tlosa dintlha tse tsebahatsang motho. Ha o download-a pokello ya data, o dumela hore o ke ke wa leka ho fumana boitsebiso ba mang kapa mang ba bafani. Hoo ho bolela hore o ke ke wa leka ho hokanya dintlha tse kahara pokello ya data ho dintlha tsa boitsebiso tsa botho tsa mofani. Leha ho le jwalo, o ka sebedisa pokello ya data ho kwetlisa temoho ya puo, temoho ya sebui, kapa disebediswa tse ding, mohlala, ho kopanya dintlha tse ka hara pokello ya data ho dintlha tse ding tse neng di se di le teng ka hara pokello ya data.

The Common Voice dataset is available for download under the <licenseLink>CC0</licenseLink> license on <datasetLink>our Datasets page</datasetLink>. You can also download several other publicly available datasets from the same page.

Pokello ya data ya Common Voice e ya fumaneha bakeng sa download tlasa<licenceLink>CC0</licenceLink>laecense ho<datasetLink>leqephe la Dipokello tsa data la rona</datasetLink>. O ka boela wa download-a dipokello tsa data tse ding tse ngata tse fumanehang phatlalatsa leqepheng le tshwanang.

Mobokelli wa dipolelo wa Common Voice o bokelletse { $sentenceCount } dipolelo ka { $languageCount } dipuo!

Palo ya dihora tsa ho rekota lentswe tseo re di bokeletseng ho fihlela jwale.

The goal of the Common Voice dataset is to enable anyone in the world to build speech recognition, speaker recognition, or any other type of application that requires voice data. A voice assistant is just one of many types of applications you could use the dataset to build.

Sepheo sa pokello ya data ya Common Voice ke ho nolofalletsa mang le mang lefatsheng ho haha sesebediswa se sebetsang ka ho elellwa puo, ho elellwa sebui, kapa mofuta o feng kapa o feng wa sesebediswa o hlokang data ya mantswe. Mothusi wa lentswe ke e nngwe ya mefuta e mengata ya disebediswa tseo o ka sebedisang pokello ya data ho di haha.

The multi-language version of the Common Voice dataset is currently undergoing community supported bundling and cleaning. If you would like to help us bring Common Voice to new languages, go check out the <sentenceCollectorLink>Sentence Collection Tool</sentenceCollectorLink> for adding new sentences to the dataset, and Mozilla <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> for translating the website itself. New languages are added to Common Voice for voice contribution when 5000 approved sentences have been collected.

Mofuta wa dipuo tse ngata wa pokello ya data wa Common Voice hajwale o ntse o kopanngwa le ho hlwekiswa ke setjhaba. Haeba o ka thabela ho re thusa ho tlisa Common Voice ho dipuo tse ntjha, tsa mo sheba <sentenceCollectorLink>Sesebediswa se Bokellang Dipolelo</sentenceCollectorLink> bakeng sa ho kenya dipolelo tse ntjha ho pokello ya data, le ho Mozilla <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> bakeng sa ho toloka sebaka sa marang-rang ka bo sona. Dipuo tse ntjha di kenyelletswa ho Common Voice bakeng sa tlatsetso ya lentswe ha dipolello tse 5000 tse amohetsweng di se di bokelletswe.
The number of recordings and which languages you contribute to will be public.
Palo ya dikgatiso le dipuo tseo o kenyang letsoho ho tsona di tla phatlalatswa.
The process by which a contributor’s profile information is obscured from their donated voice clips when packaged for download as a part of the dataset.
Mokgwa oo ka oona dintlha tsa profaele ya motlatsi di patilweng ho dikotwana tsa lentswe tseo a faneng ka tsona ha di pakelwa bakeng sa ho daonlouda e le karolo ya pokello ya data.

There are a lot of public organizations that might want to collaborate and already have a lot of texts they can donate as public domain. Reach out to local universities, Governments and open source organizations to talk about the project and ask for their help.

Ho na le mekgatlo e mengata ya setjhaba e ka batlang ho sebedisana mme e se e ntse e e-na le dingolwa tse ngata tseo ba ka fanang ka tsona setjhabeng. Ikopanye le diunivesithi tsa lehae, Mebuso le mekgatlo ya tse buletsweng bohle ho bua ka morero le ho kopa thuso ya bona.

There are lots of ways to think about language. For the purposes of speech recognition models, Common Voice suggests focussing on ‘mutual intelligibility’, or ‘can speakers of this language mostly understand one another if they try to?’

Ho na le mekgwa e mengata ya ho nahana ka puo. Bakeng sa di-model tse elellwang puo, Common Voice e kgothaletsa ho tsepamisa maikutlo ho 'kutlwisisano' kapa ‘na dibui tsa puo ee di ya utlwisisana boholo ha di leka ho etsa jwalo?'
Rekgoto e ne e le telele haholo.
Rekoto e ne e thotse haholo.
Rekoto e ne e le kgutshwanyane haholo.

There will be natural variations in volume between readers. Reject only if the volume is so high that the recording breaks up, or (more commonly) if it is so low that you can’t hear what is being said without reference to the written text.

Ho tla ba le mefuta e fapaneng ya tlhaho ya bophahamo ba modumo pakeng tsa babadi. Hana ha feela bophahamo ba modumo bo le hodimo hoo rekoto e kgaohang, kapa (ho tlwaelehileng haholo) o le tlase hoo o sa utlweng se buuwang ntle le ho sheba sengolwa.

These languages are currently under community development. <localizationGlossaryLink>Website localization</localizationGlossaryLink> and <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>sentence collection</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink> is needed to launch.

Dipuo tsena hajwale di tlasa ntshetsopele ya setjhaba. <localizationGlossaryLink> Localization ya sebaka sa Marang-rang</localizationGlossaryLink> le <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>pokello ya dipolelo</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink> dia hlokahala bakeng sa ho qala.

The Sentence Collector is part of <commonVoiceLink>Common Voice</commonVoiceLink>. It allows contributors to collect and validate sentences created by the community. You can use this tool also to import and clean-up small-to-medium-sized public domain corpus you have found or collected. All sentences need to be Public Domain. Approved sentences are exported every week to the Common Voice repository and are released on the Common Voice website on every new deployment.

Mmokelli wa dipolelo ke karolo ya<commonVoiceLink>Common Voice</commonVoiceLink>. E dumella bafani ho bokella le ho netefatsa dipolelo tse entsweng ke setjhaba. O ka sebedisa sesebediswa sena ho tlisa kahare le ho hlwekisa dingolwa tsa setjhaba tse nyane ho isa ho tse bohareng tseo o di fumaneng kapa o di bokelletseng. Dipolelo kaofela di tlameha hore ebe Tsa Setjhaba. Dipolelo tse amohetsweng di romellwa beke e nngwe le e nngwe ho polokelo ya Common Voice mme di ntshetswa setsing sa marang-rang sa Common Voice ka thomello e nngwe le e nngwe e ntjha.

The site is ready to be launched when it reaches 75% completion.
Sebaka sa marang-rang se loketse ho phatlalatswa ha se fihla ho phethwa hwa 75%.

The site will be back up as soon as possible. For the latest information, please join the <matrixLink>Matrix community chat</matrixLink> or visit <githubLink>GitHub</githubLink> or <discourseLink>our Discourse forums</discourseLink> to submit and monitor site experience issues.

Sebaka sa marang-rang se tla kgutla ka pele ka moo ho ka kgonahalang. Bakeng sa dintlha tsa morao, ka kopo ikopanye le <matrixLink>moqoqo wa setjhaba sa Matrix</matrixLink> kapa etela <githubLink>GitHub</githubLink> kapa <discourseLink>diforamo tsa rona tsa Discourse</discourseLink> ho romella le ho hlokomela ditaba tsa boiphihlelo ba sebaka sa marang-rang.
The TED-LIUM corpus was made from audio talks and their transcriptions available on the TED website.
TED-LIUM corpus e entswe ho tswa ho dipuo tsa audio mme dingodilweng tsa tsona di fumaneha marang-rang a TED.
The website text is translated into that language.
Mongolo wa sebaka sa Marang-rang o fetolelwa puong eo.
This is approximately the number of hours required to train a production speech-to-text system.
Ena e batla e le kakanyo ya palo ya dihora tse hlokahalang ho kwetlisa tlhahiso ya sistimi ya puo-ho-ya-ho-mongolo.
This is a use case driven segment containing data to power spoken digit recognition and yes / no detection.
Ena ke karolo e tsamaiswang ke use case e nang le data ho matlafatsa temoho ya palo e buuweng le temoho ya ee / tjhe.
This is our process for translating and adapting our content for many locales (languages).
Ena ke tshebetso ya rona ya ho fetolela le ho fetola ditaba tsa rona bakeng sa dibaka tse ngata (dipuo).

This page gives you an overview of all your submitted sentences. You may also delete already submitted sentences if needed, by marking the checkbox next to it and clicking on "Delete selected sentences" at the bottom. Please only remove sentences if absolutely necessary, for example if you noticed after the fact that a sentence is copyright protected.

Leqephe lena le o fa kakaretso ya dipolelo kaofela tseo o di rometseng. O ka boela wa hlakola dipolelo tse seng di rometswe ha ho hlokahala, ka ho tshwaya lebokose le haufi le tsona ebe o tobetsa"Phumula dipolelo tse kgethilweng" ka tlase. Ka kopo tlosa feela dipolelo ha ho hlokahala, mohlala haeba o hlokometse kamorao hore polelo e sireleditswe ke molao wa ditokelo tsa dingolwa.

This setting controls your leaderboard visibility. When hidden, your progress will be private. This means your image, user name and progress will not appear on the leaderboard. Note that leaderboard refresh takes ~{ $minutes }min to populate changes.

Tlhophiso ena e laola ponahalo ya boto-ya-baetapele. Ha e patehile, tswelopele ya hao e tla ba lekunutu. Hona ho bolela hore setshwantsho sa hao, lebitso la mosebedisi le tswelopele ya hao ha di na hlaha ho boto-ya-baetapele. Hlokomela hore ntjhafatso ya boto-ya-baetapele e nka ~{ $minutes } metsoso ho bontsha diphetoho.
Tse tharo tse tlang ho tsamaea!

Tshebetso ya kajeno ya Common Voice di-cliping tsa mantswe e netefaditswe
To make it into the Common Voice dataset, a voice clip must be validated by two separate users.
Ho kengwa ka hara pokello ya data ya Common Voice, clip ya lentswe e tlameha ho netefatswa ke basebedisi ba babedi ba arohaneng.

To make the Common Voice dataset as useful as possible we have decided to only allow source text that is available under a Creative Commons (CC0) license. Using the CC0 standard means its more difficult to find and collect source text, but allows anyone to use the resulting voice data without usage restrictions or authorization from Mozilla. Ultimately, we want to make the multi-language dataset as useful as possible to everyone, including researchers, universities, startups, governments, social purpose organizations, and hobbyists.

Ho etsa hore pokello ya data ya Common Voice e be molemo ka moo ho ka kgonehang re nkile qeto ya ho dumella sengolwa-sa-mathomo se fumanehang feela tlasa laesense ya Creative Commons (CC0). Ho sebedisa maemo a CC0 ho bolela hore ho thata ho fumana le ho bokella sengolwa-sa-mathomo, empa ho dumella mang kapa mang ho sebedisa data ya mantswe ntle le dithibelo tsa tshebediso kapa tumello ho tswa ho Mozilla. Qetellong, re batla ho etsa hore pokello ya data ya puo tse ngata e be molemo haholo ka moo ho ka kgonehang ho bohle, ho kenyelletsa bafuputsi,diunivesithi, ba qalang dikgwebo, mebuso, mekgatlo ya merero ya setjhaba, le batho ba ratang ho itlosa bodutu.

Translation is from English, but you can see Suggestions in other languages. Click the Profile icon, then Settings link and add any languages you speak. At the bottom right-hand corner will be a list of translations called Locales. Translations show on the site after one day.

Qetellong k'honeng ya letsoho le letona ho tla ba le lenane la diphetolelo tse bitswang Locales. Diphetolelo di bontshwa setsing sa marang-rang kamora letsatsi le le leng.

Validating donated clips is equally important to the Common Voice mission. Take a listen and help us create quality open source voice data.
Ho netefatsa dikotwana tse fanweng ho bohlokwa ka ho lekana ho sepheo sa Common Voice. Mamela mme o re thuse ho etsa data ya mantswe e buletsweng bohle e nang le boleng.
Variants are a specific form of a language - for example shared by those living in a geography or commmunity. Sometimes these are called dialects.
Variants ke mofuta o itseng wa puo - mohlala, e arolelalanwa ke ba phelang tikolohong kapa setjhabeng. Ka dinako tse ding tsena di bitswa maleme.

Pokello ya Lentswe, Itokisetse ho download-a

Voice is natural, voice is human. That’s why we’re excited about creating usable voice technology for our machines. But to create voice systems, developers need an extremely large amount of voice data.

Lentswe ke la tlhaho, lentswe ke la motho. Ke ka lebaka leo re thabetseng ho etsa thekenoloji ya lentswe bakeng sa metjhini ya rona. Empa ho etsa ditsamaiso tsa mantswe, bahahi ba marang-rang ba hloka data ya mantswe e kgolo haholo.
Voice is natural, voice is human. That’s why we’re fascinated with creating usable voice technology for our machines. But to create voice systems, an extremely large amount of voice data is required.

Lentswe ke la tlhaho, lentswe ke la motho. Ke ka lebaka leo re hloletsweng ke ho etsa thekenoloji ya lentswe bakeng sa metjhini ya rona. Empa ho etsa ditsamaiso tsa mantswe, data e kgolo haholo e ya hlokahala.

Voice recognition technology is revolutionizing the way we interact with machines, but the currently available systems are expensive and proprietary. Common Voice is part of Mozilla’s initiative to make voice recognition technologies better and more accessible for everyone. Common Voice is a massive global database of donated voices that lets anyone quickly and easily train voice-enabled apps in potentially every language.

Thekenoloji e elellwang lentswe e fetola tsela yeo re sebedisanang le metjhini ka yona, empa ditsamaiso tse teng hona jwale di turu ebile di ruilwe ke batho ba itseng. Common Voice ke karolo ya boikitlaetso ba Mozilla ba ho etsa hore thekenoloji e elellwang lentswe e be betere le hore e fumanehe ho bohle. Common Voice ke database e kgolo haholo ya lefatshe ka bophara ya mantswe a nehetsweng e dumellang mang kapa mang hore a kwetlise ka potlako le ka bonolo disebediswa tse sebetsang ka lentswe ho puo e nngwe le e nngwe.

Voice recognition technology is revolutionizing the way we interact with machines, but the currently available systems are expensive and proprietary. Mozilla Common Voice is an initiative to make voice recognition technologies better and more accessible for everyone. Common Voice is a massive global database of donated voices that lets anyone quickly and easily train voice-enabled apps in potentially every language.
We're not only collecting voice samples in widely spoken languages but also in those with a smaller population of speakers. Publishing a diverse dataset of voices will empower developers, entrepreneurs, and communities to address this gap themselves.

Thekenoloji e elellwang lentswe e fetola tsela yeo re sebedisanang le metjhini ka yona, empa ditsamaiso tse teng hona jwale di turu ebile di ruilwe ke batho ba itseng. Common Voice ke karolo ya boikitlaetso ba Mozilla ba ho etsa hore thekenoloji e elellwang lentswe e be betere le hore e fumanehe ho bohle. Common Voice ke database e kgolo haholo ya lefatshe ka bophara ya mantswe a nehetsweng e dumellang mang kapa mang hore a kwetlise ka potlako le ka bonolo disebediswa tse sebetsang ka lentswe ho puo e nngwe le e nngwe.
Ha re bokelle feela mehlala ya mantswe a dipuo tse buuwang haholo empa le ho dipuo tse buuwang ke ditjhaba tse fokolang ka palo. Ho phatlalatsa pokello ya data e mefuta-futa ya mantswe ho tla matlafatsa dihahi tsa marang-rang, bahwebi, le ditjhaba kaofela tsa puo ho sebetsana le sekgeo sena ka bo bona.
VoxForge was set up to collect transcribed speech for use with Free and Open Source Speech Recognition Engines.
VoxForge e thehilwe ho bokella puo e ngotsweng hore e sebediswe le dienjene tsa Mahala mme tsa Mohlodi o Bulehileng wa Temoho ya Puo.

Want to help make Common Voice even better? Great! Get in touch via email or <discourseLink>Discourse</discourseLink> forums, submit site issues via <githubLink>GitHub</githubLink>, or join the <matrixLink>Matrix</matrixLink> community chat.

O batla ho dula o buisana le Common Voice?

Na o batla ditaba tsa morao-rao ha re lokolla mofuta o motjha wa pokello ya data ya Common Voice? Ngodisa ho koranta ya rona.

Shebella setsebi sa rona sa video ho o thusa
We are building an open and publicly available dataset of voices that everyone can use to train speech-enabled applications.
Re aha pokello ya data e bulehileng le e fumanehang phatlalatsa ya mantswe eo motho e mong le e mong a ka e sebedisang ho kwetlisa diaplikeishene tse dumelang lentswe.
We are going out to get a coffee.
We are going out to get coffee.
We at Mozilla are building a community around voice technology. We would like to stay in touch with updates, new data sources and to hear more about how you're using this data.

Rona ba Mozilla re haha setjhaba mabapi le thekenoloji e sebetsang ka lentswe. Re ka lakatsa ho dula re tseba ka dintlafatso, mehlodi e metjha ya data le ho utlwa haholwanyane ka hore na o sebedisa data ena jwang.
We at Mozilla are building a community around voice technology. We would like to stay in touch with updates, new data sources and to hear more about how you're using this data.

Rona ba Mozilla re haha setjhaba mabapi le thekenoloji e sebetsang ka lentswe. Re ka lakatsa ho dula re tseba ka dintlafatso, mehlodi e metjha ya data le ho utlwa haholwanyane ka hore na o sebedisa data ena jwang.

We believe that large and publicly available voice datasets foster innovation and healthy commercial competition in machine-learning based speech technology. This is a global effort and we invite everyone to participate. Our aim is to help speech technology be more inclusive, reflecting the diversity of voices from around the world.

Re dumela hore dipokello tsa data tse kgolo le tse fumanehang phatlalatsa di kgothaletsa dintho tse ntjha le tlhodisano e ntle ya dikgwebo ho thekenoloji ya puo e thehilweng holima ho ithuta motjhini. Hona ke matsapa a lefatshe ka bophara mme re mema bohle ho nka karolo. Sepheo sa rona ke ho thusa thekenoloji ya puo e kenyelletse bongata, e bonahatse phapano ya mantswe a tswang lefatsheng.

We believe that large, publicly available voice datasets will foster innovation and healthy commercial competition in machine-learning based speech technology.
Common Voice’s multi-language dataset is already the largest publicly available voice dataset of its kind, but it’s not the only one.
Look to this page as a reference hub for other open source voice datasets and, as Common Voice continues to grow, a home for our release updates.

Re dumela hore dipokello tsa data tse kgolo le tse fumanehang phatlalatsa di kgothaletsa dintho tse ntjha le tlhodisano e ntle ya dikgwebo ho thekenoloji ya puo e thehilweng hodima ho ithuta motjhini.
Pokello ya data ya dipuo tse ngata ya Common Voice e se ntse e le yona e kgolo ka ho fetisisa e fumanehang phatlalatsa ya mofuta wa yona, empa ha se yona feela.
Sheba leqepheng lena e le setsi sa tshupiso bakeng sa dipokello tsa data tsa lentswe tse ding tse buletsweng setjhaba hape e le, ha Common Voice e ntse e tswela-pele ho hola, lehae la rona la tokollo ya dintlafatso.

We calculate hours by estimating the average length of each recording, and then multiplying that number by the total number of recordings across all languages.
Re bala dihora ka ho hakanya karolelano ya bolele ba rekoto ka nngwe, mme re ngatafatse nomoro eo ka kakaretso ya nomoro ya direkoto dipuong tsohle.

We prefer natural/conversational sentences. While phonetic diversity and different words in sentences is important, we are trying to make recording sentences as much fun as possible. Therefore it would be great if you could try to keep your sentences as natural/engaging as possible.

Re thabela dipolelo tsa tlhaho/moqoqo. Leha mefuta e fapaneng ya difonetiki le mantswe a fapaneng kahara dipolelo a le bohlokwa, re leka ho etsa ho rekota ha dipolelo ho be monate ka moo ho ka kgonehang. Kahoo ho ka ba hantle ha o ka leka ho etsa hore dipolelo tsa hao ebe tse hohelang kamoo ho ka kgonahalang.

We’re building an open source, multi-language dataset of voices that anyone can use to train speech-enabled applications.
Re theha pokello ya data ya mantswe ya dipuo tse ngata e mohlodi o bulehileng, eo mang kapa mang a ka e sebedisang ho kwetlisa diaplikeishene tse sebetsang ka puo.
We’re crowdsourcing an open-source dataset of voices. Donate your voice, validate the accuracy of other people’s clips, make the dataset better for everyone.

Re etsa pokello ya data ya mantswe e tswang ho batho ba bangata e buletsweng bohle. Nehela ka lentswe la hao, netefatsa nepahalo ya dikotwana tsa batho ba bang, etsa pokello ya data e betere bakeng sa bohle.

We use a Mozilla tool called <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> for translations. Pontoon has lots of languages, but if it doesn’t have yours you can <pontoonRequestLink>request for your language to be added</pontoonRequestLink>. Then, to make the language available on the Common Voice project, <githubRepoLink>request the new language on GitHub</githubRepoLink>. <localizationFragment>See more on site translation</localizationFragment>.

Re sebedisa sesebediswa sa Mozilla se bitswang <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> bakeng sa diphetolelo. Pontoon e na le dipuo tse ngata, empa ha e sena puo ya hao o ka <pontoonRequestLink>kopa hore puo ya hao e kenyelletswe</pontoonRequestLink>. Ebe, ho etsa hore puo e fumanehe ho morero wa Common Voice, <githubRepoLink>kopa puo e ntjha ho GitHub</githubRepoLink>. <localizationFragment>Sheba tse ding hape ho setsi sa marang-rang sa botoloki</localizationFragment>.
We've run out of clips to validate in this language...
Re feletswe ke di-clip tse lokelang ho netefatswa ho puo ena...
We've run out of sentences to record in this language...
Re feletswe ke dipolelo tseo re lokelang ho di rekota ka puo ena...
We want speech models to be better at understanding a diverse range of speakers. For this to happen, a voice dataset must represent lots of different people.
Re batla hore di-model tsa puo di be betere ho utlwisisa mefuta e fapaneng ya dibui. Ho re sena se etsahale, pokello ya data ya lentswe e tlameha ho emela batho ba bangata ba fapaneng.

We want the Common Voice dataset to reflect the audio quality a speech-to-text engine will hear in the wild, so we’re looking for variety. In addition to a diverse community of speakers, a dataset with varying audio quality will teach the speech-to-text engine to handle various real-world situations, from background talking to car noise. As long as your voice clip is intelligible, it should be good enough for the dataset.

Re batla hore pokello ya data ya Common Voice e bonahatse boleng ba audio boo enjene ya puo-ho-ya-ho-sengolwa e tla e utlwa hara thota, kahoo re batla mefuta e fapaneng. Ka tlatselletsa ho mefuta-futa ya ditjhaba tsa dibui, pokello ya data e nang le di-audio tse boleng bo fapaneng e tla ruta enjene ya puo-ho-ya-ho-sengolwa ho sebetsana le maemo a lefatshe a fapaneng, ho tloha ho dipuisano tse kamorao ho isa ho lerata la koloi. Ha feela sekotwana sa lentswe sa hao se utlwahala, se tla be se lokile ho lekana bakeng sa pokello ya data.

We want the machine learning algorithms to able to handle a variety of background noise, and even relatively loud noises can be accepted provided that they don’t prevent you from hearing the entirety of the text. Quiet background music is OK; music loud enough to prevent you from hearing each and every word is not.

Re batla hore di-algorithm tsa ho ithuta motjhini di kgone ho sebetsana le mefuta e fapaneng ya lerata le kamorao, mme le marata a hodimo a ka amohelwa ha feela a sa o setisi hore o utlwe sengolwa se felletseng. Lerata la mmino le kamorao le thotseng le nepahetse; mmino o phahameng haholo o sitisang hore o utlwe lentswe le leng le le leng wona ha wa nepahala.
We will also let our community manager know that you’re interested, so they can give you any extra support or information you might need.
Hape re tla tsebisa mookamedi wa setjhaba sa rona hore o na le thahasello, kahoo ba ka o fa tshehetso e feng kapa feng e eketsehileng kapa dintlha tseo o ka di hlokang.
We will be in touch with more information about how to add your language to Common Voice very soon.
Re tla ikopanya ka dintlha tse ding mabapi le mokgwa wa ho kenya puo ya hao ho Common Voice haufinyane.
We will be in touch with more information as it becomes available.
Re tla ikopanya le wena ka dintlha tse ding ha di ntse di fumaneha.
We will not make your email public.
Re ke ke ra etsa hore imeile ya hao e phatlalatswe.

We will review your request to remove your voice recordings from the dataset. If your request is approved, we will contact those who have downloaded the dataset and request they remove your voice recordings as well.

Re tla hlahloba kopo ya hao ya ho tlosa direkoto tsa lentswe la hao ho pokello ya data. Haeba kopo ya hao e amohelehile, re tla ikopanya le ba neng ba se ba download-ile pokello ya data mme re kope hore ba tlose direkoto tsa lentswe la hao le bona.

When a user rejects a voice clip it returns to the Queue. If rejected a second time, the voice clip is moved to the Clip Graveyard.
Ha mosebedisi a hana sekotwana sa lentswe e kgutlela Lethathamong. Haeba e hanwa lekgetlo la bobedi, sekotwana sa lentswe e iswa ho Lebitla la dikotwana.

When listening, check very carefully that what has been recorded is exactly what has been written; reject if there are even minor errors.

Ha o mametse, sheba ka hloko hore se rekotilweng ke sona hantle se ngotsweng; hana haeba ho na le diphoso le tse nyane.
Diphoso tse tlwaelehileng haholo di kenyelletsa:

Na o ka rata ho kopa direkoto tsa mantswe tsa hao di hlakolwe, kapa o kgetha ho di boloka kahara pokello ya data ya Common Voice?
Yes, send me emails. I’d like to stay informed about the Common Voice Project.

Yes, send me emails. I'd like to stay informed about the progress of this language on Common Voice.

Yes, we especially want your voice! Part of the aim of Common Voice is to gather as many different accents as possible so that voice recognition services work equally well for everyone. This means donations from non-native speakers are particularly important.

E, re batla haholoholo lentswe la hao! Karolo e nngwe ya sepheo sa Common Voice ke ho bokella ditsela tsa puo tse ngata tse fapaneng hore ditshebeletso tsa temoho ya mantswe di sebetse hantle ka mokgwa o tshwanang ho bohle. Hona ho bolela hore menehelo e tswang ho batho bao e seng matswallwa are bohlokwa ho feta.
